title: Build a custom booking engine using Monday.com and 'XXYY' software
excerpt:

posts.author: MarkHHH (Mark Laurence)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday community,

We are an education company in New Zealand, that uses Monday.com (very happily!) primarily for the following 2 reasons:

  • Our CRM
  • To visually show the timetables of our lessons to our tutors, so they can see the students in their classes.

Currently we use a very manual process of enrolling students. This is becoming unwieldy as we grow.

I’m looking for an expert to help us through the process of building a booking engine, using an API between Monday.com and some as-yet-undiscovered booking software that would fit our business’ use case.

The project would involve:

  • Researching the most appropriate booking software for our use case.
  • Understanding how we build our lesson timetables in Monday.com
  • Understanding how we use Monday.com as our CRM
  • Developing the API to connect the booking software and Monday.com
  • Embedding the front-end tool into our website.
  • Other steps that I likely can’t account for given my naivety in these areas.

Really keen to hear from anyone out there who feels they can take this project on with us!

Regards,
Mark

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @MarkHHH!

I am really happy that you reached out to the community.
We at Omnitas Consulting are a Swedish digital management consultancy firm and official Preferred Partner for monday.com. With the aid of monday.com, Omnitas help companies of all sizes and in all types of markets to map, digitalise, and optimise their workflows and processes.
in plain text: We are a 4 man team whom have worked full time for the past 3 years with implementing monday.com and designing processes and integrations around the plattform. I would dare to say that there are very few usecases we can not solve with monday.com as the base.

We have worked with a Swedish education company before with great results. monday.com works great as a educational planning tool and I am happy to learn that you are using monday.com as a CRM, that is one of our strengths.

That being said I would like to start the cooperation of with a initial scoping session.

We are around 10 hours behind New Zeland. I do not see this as a problem. We can work around that as long as we have our meetings early your time (first thing in the morning). Please send me an invite to thomas@omnitas.se with a video meeting sometimes this or next week for 8 or 9 in the morning your time.

The call will be around 30-60 minutes (free) and we will talk about your needs and what you want to achieve in greater detail. This will give me an idea if we can help you or not and provide me with the correct information for a proper quote.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: Roib22198 (Roi Bar On)
posts.cookedHtml:
@MarkHHH I’m a Channel Partners Manager at monday.com and I work closely with Omnitas. Their knowledge of the monday platform and professionalism is unique. I highly recommend them for this project.

Best of luck!

posts.author: Joe_Bushnell (Joe Bushnell)
posts.cookedHtml: @MarkHHH We are the UK Partner for monday.com and have worked closely on numerous projects with @Thomas-Omnitas and his team at Omnitas. You would be in probably the best monday.com hands worldwide if you selected to go with them. Hope your project goes smoothly. Thanks Joe @ Enable

posts.author: NManos (Nick Manos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thomas,

We would be interested in hearing more about your expertise.

We are a similarly sized company that sees MONDAY.com as a pathway to our future for many customers.

Nick

posts.author: MarkHHH (Mark Laurence)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Thomas-Omnitas Thomas, thanks so much for your detailed reply. Also thanks to you for your generous offer of a first-up video call to show you what we’re doing and how we’d like to use Monday in more advanced ways. I’ll send you an email now.

Thanks also to @Joe_Bushnell and @Roib22198 for your kind recommendations of Omintas’s services, they mean a lot and make me really enthused about the possibilities ahead

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml: Sounds good. Looking forward to chatting with you tomorrow!

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would love to talk about your use case(s) and how we can help.
send me an email with some timings (thomas@omnitas.se).

posts.author: Overnhou (Overnhou)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there! While I’ve had some experience with Monday.com and API integrations, diving into a project this intricate may require expert guidance. I recommend breaking it down into smaller steps and conducting thorough research. Checking out the Monday.com community forums could provide valuable insights. Good luck!
